items.definition_en: The complete amount of rain that falls during a specific period.
items.example_en: The rainfall total for June was higher than usual.

items.definition_en: A weather event where rain freezes on surfaces, creating layers of ice.
items.example_en: Driving was dangerous after last night's ice storm.

items.definition_en: A storm characterized by a large amount of falling snow and strong wind.
items.example_en: Many flights were canceled due to the snowstorm.

items.definition_en: Small, soft balls of snow covered with ice that fall during cold weather.
items.example_en: The ground was covered with graupel after the storm.

items.definition_en: Partly melted snow or ice, usually mixed with water.
items.example_en: The sidewalks were full of dirty slush.

items.definition_en: A deep pile of snow formed by the wind.
items.example_en: We had to walk around a high snowdrift.

items.definition_en: A narrow area of heavy rainfall, usually associated with storms.
items.example_en: A rainband moved across the city in the afternoon.

items.definition_en: A rare weather event where thunder occurs during a snowstorm.
items.example_en: Thundersnow surprised the residents last night.

items.definition_en: A small piece of ice that forms naturally in the air or on surfaces.
items.example_en: The window was covered with tiny ice crystals.

items.definition_en: Rain that falls so heavily it looks like a solid sheet.
items.example_en: We could barely see through the sheeting rain.

items.definition_en: A long, thin piece of ice hanging from a surface.
items.example_en: An icicle dangled from the edge of the roof.

items.definition_en: Water that comes from rain and is collected or found on the ground.
items.example_en: They collect rainwater to water their plants.

items.definition_en: Snow that has a high water content and feels heavy.
items.example_en: It is hard to shovel all the wet snow from the driveway.

items.definition_en: A small amount of snow falling at a gentle rate.
items.example_en: There was light snow in the morning.

items.definition_en: A brief, sudden, and intense burst of rain and wind.
items.example_en: A rain squall caught us by surprise at the park.

items.definition_en: Snow that is changing into water because of warmer temperatures.
items.example_en: The streets are wet with melting snow.

items.definition_en: A frost that is cold enough to freeze the ground and other surfaces firmly.
items.example_en: Crops were damaged by last night's hard frost.

items.definition_en: The total layer of snow lying on the ground.
items.example_en: Snow cover was deep after the storm.

items.definition_en: A large flat area of ice covering the ground.
items.example_en: An ice sheet made walking dangerous.

items.definition_en: A weather condition where snowfall makes it impossible to see clearly.
items.example_en: Driving in a whiteout is very risky.
